Point A (maximum point on the curve)
spatial_evidence: The curve starts at the origin, rises to a maximum at A, then decreases to a minimum at (b, 0), and increases again
The shaded region is located between the line OA and the curve from x = 0 to x = a
The line OA is straight and connects O to A

diagram_elements: semicircles with diameters PQ and RS
spatial_evidence: The plate is symmetrical about the vertical axis through C.
Points P and Q are at the top of the upper semicircle.
Points R and S are at the bottom of the lower semicircle.
The two removed sections are symmetrically placed above and below the center C.

diagram_elements: Shaded region bounded by the curve and lines
spatial_evidence: The shaded region lies between x = 3 and x = 5, bounded below by y = 1 and above by the curve y² = x - 2.
The origin (0,0) is shown, with the x-axis extending to 5 and the y-axis marked at 1.

diagram_elements: six surrounding circles labeled B, C, D, E, F
points P and Q on the central circle
rope wrapping around the outer edges of the circles
spatial_evidence: symmetrical arrangement of six outer circles around one central circle
equal spacing between centers of outer circles
rope following external tangents between adjacent circles
angle PAQ subtended by arc PQ on the central circle
BD is drawn from B to AC, forming a right angle at D
The arc BC is part of the circle centered at A
Points A, B, and C form the sector, with AB and AC as radii
Point A is located to the left of point B along the x-axis
The curve lies below the line between points A and B
The shaded region between the curve and the line is bounded by points A and B

diagram_elements: tick marks on x-axis labeled 0, π, 2π, 3π, 4π
spatial_evidence: The diagram provides a blank coordinate system for sketching a curve.
The x-axis is scaled in multiples of π up to 4π.
No curve or additional shapes are drawn on the diagram.
Arc AB connects points A and B, forming a curved segment from the larger circle
Arc CD connects points C and D, forming a curved segment from the smaller circle
Right angle at point O between CO and OD
θ is the angle at O between BO and AO
The curve starts at the origin and bends downward before rising again.
The line AB is straight and crosses the curve at two points, A and B.
The shaded region is enclosed between the curve and the line, indicating the area to be calculated.

diagram_elements: angles ABF and DCE labeled as 1/3π rad
points A, B, C, D, E, F
spatial_evidence: ABCD is straight and horizontal
sectors ABF and DCE are on opposite ends of the plate
EF is the bottom edge connecting F and E
symmetry implies ABF and DCE are mirror images
The shaded region is bounded on the left by the curve y^2 = 1 - 2x and on the right by the curve y = (2x - 1)^2.
Point A is at the origin where both curves pass through (0, 0).
Point B is above the x-axis and lies on both curves.

diagram_elements: Vertices labeled A, B, C, D
Edges connecting vertices: AB, AC, AD, BC, BD, CD
spatial_evidence: The diagram shows a three-dimensional structure with point D elevated above the plane of triangle ABC
